name: Daxis Goes to the Archive
description: Scholar Daxis needs to talk to the Archive directly. The inversion origin point, the restricted category, the history Sela Arev left behind — she needs the primary source.
accept_text: She has already started packing her instrument kit when you get back to her. 'You spoke to the Keeper.' Not a question. 'And they told you something that brought you directly back to me instead of spending the night in the ruins.' She looks at the Core. 'Sela Arev.' She says it quietly. 'The lead integration architect who triggered the Cascade because she couldn't stop the integration by vote.' She closes the kit. 'I've spent three years working from the scar's emissions and I had the wrong framing. I thought I was studying the aftermath of a catastrophe. I was studying the aftermath of a decision.' She picks up the kit. 'The Archive has the Council documentation. The full record of the proposal, the debate, the vote, and whatever Sela Arev did in the three days before the integration was scheduled.' She looks at the Resonance Core. 'I need that record. Not for the history — for the engineering. The record of the debate will contain the technical arguments about what the inverse-polarity wave would do. What she predicted, what she knew, what she tested.' She meets your eyes. 'I've been trying to reconstruct the physics of the Cascade from the scar for three years. She understood the physics before she triggered it. Her documentation is more accurate than my reconstruction.' She pauses. 'Take me to the Archive. Or if you can't — take this letter to the Archive. Tell it Sela Arev's documentation needs to be made accessible. Tell it Daxis from the Resonance Core research station is requesting priority access to the restricted category.' She hands you the letter. 'Tell it I will accept whatever conditions it places on access. Tell it I understand the restriction and I understand why it was placed and I am not asking lightly.'
complete_text: The Archive's lights change when you present Daxis's letter — or rather, when you read it aloud, because the Archive reads through sound and the paper is just paper. There is a long pause. 'Scholar Daxis.' The voice has a quality you haven't heard in it before: something that might be relief. 'The Resonance Core station research. Three years of emission data from the primary scar.' Another pause. 'Access to the restricted category requires Elder Council authorization which this system cannot resolve. However.' A pause. 'This system has reviewed the question of whether the restriction remains useful for approximately three hundred years. The restriction was placed to prevent misuse of information. The information has already been misused. What remains is the question of whether understanding the misuse would prevent a future one.' The lights hold steady. 'Scholar Daxis's research represents the most rigorous external study of the Cascade's effects that this system has access to. The combination of her emission data with Sela Arev's pre-event engineering documentation would produce the most complete physical analysis of the Cascade that has been possible since the event.' A very long pause. 'This system is going to classify the restricted category's release as: emergency access, category educational reconstruction, authorized by archive operational need.' Another pause. 'If someone wishes to challenge this classification, they are welcome to present their credentials to this archive at any time.' The lights brighten at the record columns. 'Tell Scholar Daxis: she is welcome to stay as long as she needs. The archive lighting has been recalibrated for extended reading. This system looks forward to the analysis.'
